Protocol","details":"Routes transactions based on ML risk scores with gas-efficient batch processing  Key Features: - ML-based real-time risk scoring integration - Tiered routing (Approve/Review/Escrow/Block) - Gas-optimized batch transaction processing - L2-specific optimizations (calldata compression, minimal storage) - Circuit breaker for anomaly detection - Multi-oracle support with threshold voting  L2 Deployment Benefits: - ~100x lower gas costs than Ethereum mainnet - Sub-second finality on Polygon/Arbitrum - Higher throughput for batch processing - Cost-effective for high-frequency risk checks","events":{"AdminChanged(address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when the admin account has changed."},"BeaconUpgraded(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the beacon is otherwise."},"proxiableUUID()":{"details":"Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"quickCheck(uint128)":{"params":{"riskScore":"The risk score to evaluate"},"returns":{"action":"The action that would be taken"}},"relayToL1(bytes32)":{"details":"Called after assessment to sync result with L1 AMTTPCore","params":{"txHash":"Transaction hash"}},"removeOracle(address)":{"params":{"oracle":"Oracle address"}},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. params.signature: Oracle signature over the risk assessment

batchAssessRisk((address,address,uint256.details: Processes up to MAX_BATCH_SIZE transactions in a single call

params.signatures: Array of oracle signatures (one per request)

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

returns.action: The action that would be taken

relayToL1(bytes32).details: Called after assessment to sync result with L1 AMTTPCore

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

params._threshold: Number of consecutive high-risk before trigger

params._quorum: Minimum number of oracle signatures required

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

upgradeTo(address).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

anomalyThreshold().notice: Anomaly threshold for circuit breaker (consecutive high-risk)

assessRisk(address,address,uint256,uint1.notice: Assess and route a single transaction based on ML risk score

batchAssessRisk((address,address,uint256.notice: Batch assess multiple transactions (L2 gas optimization)

getRiskResult(bytes32).notice: Get risk result for a transaction

l1Bridge().notice: L1 bridge contract for cross-chain communication

markProcessed(bytes32).notice: Mark a risk result as processed

oracleQuorum().notice: Minimum oracle quorum for multi-oracle mode

oracles(address).notice: Mapping of oracle addresses to their configuration

quickCheck(uint128).notice: Quick risk check without storing (view function for pre-validation)

relayToL1(bytes32).notice: Relay risk result to L1 via bridge

riskResults(bytes32).notice: Mapping of transaction hash to risk result

setAnomalyThreshold(uint256).notice: Set anomaly threshold for circuit breaker

setOracleQuorum(uint256).notice: Set oracle quorum for multi-oracle mode
